articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Widespread Concern**: 82% of landlords worry about the implications of the Renters' Right Act, with nearly half feeling very concerned.
- **Portfolio Shrinkage**: 75% of landlords are hesitant to expand their holdings, and 49% are considering selling or downsizing within the next two months.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords need to enhance EPC ratings to attract tenants, with a mandatory upgrade to a C rating by 2030 looming.  
- Increasing energy efficiency is crucial as tenants seek cost savings amid rising energy prices from global events like the Iran War.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Only 24% of UK landlords believe planning reforms will benefit their business, highlighting widespread skepticism.  
- Landbay's findings indicate a lack of confidence in government initiatives aimed at improving the property market.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Over 58% of hidden leaks in rental properties are found outside**, highlighting the critical need for landlords to maintain external pipework.  
- **Neglecting outside maintenance could lead to costly repairs**, risking thousands in damages for landlords.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters’ Rights Act, set to take effect on 1 May 2026, will significantly reshape the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales, enhancing tenant rights and imposing new regulations on landlords.  
- Landlords are urged to prepare for these upcoming changes, keeping in mind essential tips to ensure compliance and successful management of their rental properties.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Widespread Unpreparedness:** 84% of landlords are not ready for the implementation of the Renters’ Rights Act on May 1.  
- **Compliance Risks:** 58% of landlords face a high risk of non-compliance with the new legislation.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are shifting focus from expansion to restructuring portfolios and financing in light of slowing rental growth, as indicated by the latest ONS data.  
- The slowing demand emphasizes the importance of optimizing property portfolios rather than merely increasing inventory.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A new survey highlights that landlords and investors are driving first-time and local buyers out of the housing market.  
- Rising competition from landlords is creating significant barriers for newcomers trying to secure homes.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords urge the incoming government to develop a dedicated strategy highlighting the critical role of the private rented sector (PRS).  
- A formal recognition from the Scottish Government is essential to stem the ongoing outflow of landlords from the market.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- New HMRC regulations require tax advisors, including conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams, to register by May or risk penalties up to £10,000.  
- Landlords should be aware of these mandatory requirements to avoid potential fines and ensure compliance.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council claims that its licensing fees of £694 per property for landlords will not result in increased rental prices for tenants.  
- Concerns arise as landlords may struggle to absorb these costs, potentially impacting housing affordability.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour's Matthew Pennycook has definitively ruled out the introduction of rent controls.  
- This clear stance highlights the party's approach to housing policy ahead of upcoming elections.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- The council aims to regulate the Housing in Multiple Occupation (HMO) sector to curb unregulated practices by landlords.  
- New detailed guidelines for HMOs are in the works to create a more controlled rental environment.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A week-long campaign kicks off Monday to educate tenants on leveraging their rights under the Renters Rights Act.  
- The initiative aims to empower renters with knowledge to assert their entitlements effectively.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters Rights Act is leading to a surge in Section 21 evictions, aligning with predictions made by property expert Paul Shamplina.
- The increase in evictions raises concerns about housing stability for tenants amid changing rental regulations.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are increasing as landlords brace for the upcoming Renters Rights Act, according to new data from Hamptons.  
- The shift highlights a proactive adjustment in the rental market amid evolving regulations aimed at enhancing tenant protections.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The impending Renters Rights Act, set to become law in just 10 days, is poised to significantly impact the landscape of rental housing by limiting the viability of amateur or hobbyist landlords.  
- Experts warn that the legislation could lead to reduced rental supply, driving up prices and making it harder for tenants to find affordable housing options.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Gen Z Entrepreneurs Embrace Property**: Despite increasing regulations and taxes, a rising number of Gen Z entrepreneurs are diving into the property investment market.  
- **Resilience in Adversity**: This trend highlights a shift in investment strategies among younger generations, showcasing their determination to navigate and succeed in a challenging landscape.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Sellers are facing a challenging market, leading to price cuts, failed sales, and withdrawals from the property market, according to House Buyer Bureau's Chris Hodgkinson.  
- The current conditions indicate increased pressure on sellers, highlighting a shift in the housing landscape.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax
articles.summary:
The number of cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA) is increasing, boosting Inheritance Tax receipts.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC is intensifying its review of property valuations related to Inheritance Tax, leading to a rise in cases sent to the Valuation Office Agency.  
- This increased scrutiny is contributing to a boost in Inheritance Tax revenues.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos
articles.summary:
The tenant is seen asleep in bed in the property listing photographs and video for a terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria.
The post Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A potential buyer was shocked to discover a sleeping tenant featured in the listing photos of a £135k terrace property in Penrith, Cumbria.  
- The unexpected cameo was captured in both images and video, raising eyebrows about tenant privacy during property showings.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers
articles.summary:
Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.
The post Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Referral fees are labeled as an "unseen surcharge" that increases costs for homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.  
- The Conveyancing Task Force highlights these fees as a form of a 'hidden tax' negatively impacting affordability in the housing market.

articles.title: Lomond expands operation north of the border
articles.summary:
David Alexander, of Lomond, says Clyde Property Group will run its portfolio in the West of Scotland.
The post Lomond expands operation north of the border appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Lomond expands its operations into Scotland, with Clyde Property Group managing its portfolio in the West.  
- David Alexander announces strategic move to strengthen presence in the northern UK market.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.
The post Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35% appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Foxtons reports a staggering 35% drop in sales revenue, attributed to lower-than-expected buyer activity amid geopolitical challenges.  
- CEO Guy Gittins cites ongoing geopolitical headwinds as a critical factor impacting the property market.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices
articles.summary:
Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.
The post Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Inflation surges, particularly affecting fuel and transport costs, amid escalating Middle East tensions.  
- Broader economic impact anticipated as price increases are likely to permeate other sectors soon.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 90% of new homes are never listed on the open sales market, according to Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, CEO of Alto.  
- This trend highlights a significant disconnect in the UK property market, limiting options for potential buyers.
